Multibets

Flagged picks — this scan

⚠ Unproven. On a 9,381-game backtest (train + frozen test, at both opening and closing prices) the core vote-EV model returned negative ROI. The earlier positive result came from a 27-bet sample and did not survive the larger one. Treat these as research candidates, not value bets — see data/research/finding-2026-07-22-core-model.json. The drift-crowd signal is the only component with positive large-sample evidence, and it stays log-only until it earns promotion.

Research candidates — signals firing now

Picks selected by the research signals, shown while they earn their live record. Drift-crowd is the only component with positive large-sample evidence (frozen test: +16.8% ROI over 110 bets); the others are unvalidated. None of these drive flags until the promotion gate clears them on live results.

Track record — settled picks

Flat 1-unit stake at the first flagged price. CLV = price taken vs closing price; consistently positive CLV means the signal beats the market.

No settled predictions yet — the record builds automatically as flagged games finish.

Model calibration

All settled predictions (flagged or not), grouped by the model's estimated win probability. Well-calibrated = predicted ≈ actual.

Calibration appears after enough games settle. Vote weight in use: .
Odds are bet365 prices via Sofascore's feed and can lag the live bet365 site by minutes. Crowd votes are debiased by fanbase size and shrunk toward the de-vigged market probability; recent form nudges the estimate; drift against the pick and confirmed absences reduce the edge. The vote weight is re-fitted from settled results as history accumulates. Screening tool, not betting advice.
